source.definition: A series of digital audio or video files made available on the internet, often available for subscription.

explanation.sourceText: The word “podcast” was coined in the early 2000s as a blend of “iPod” (Apple’s portable music player) and “broadcast” (radio transmission).
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul „Podcast” a apărut la începutul anilor 2000 ca o combinație între „iPod” (playerul portabil de muzică de la Apple) și „Broadcast” (radiodifuziune).

target.definition: O serie de fișiere audio sau video digitale, publicate pe internet și de obicei disponibile prin abonament.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian term “podcast” is a direct borrowing from English, retaining the original form and meaning related to the combination of “iPod” and “broadcast”.
explanation.targetText: Termenul românesc „podcast” este un împrumut direct din limba engleză, păstrând forma și sensul original, legat de combinația dintre „iPod” și „broadcast”.

examples.sourceText: I listen to a podcast every morning at breakfast.
examples.targetText: Ascult un podcast în fiecare dimineață la micul dejun.

examples.sourceText: The podcast covers topics related to technology and science.
examples.targetText: Podcastul tratează subiecte legate de tehnologie și știință.

examples.sourceText: Many podcasts are free and can be downloaded anytime.
examples.targetText: Multe podcasturi sunt gratuite și pot fi descărcate oricând.

examples.sourceText: She started her own podcast to talk about books.
examples.targetText: Ea a început propriul podcast pentru a vorbi despre cărți.

learningTips.text: Remember that “cast” in podcast means to send or broadcast, which helps you understand it as a digital show.
Think of “pod” as a small container and “cast” as throwing—imagine throwing little audio containers into the internet.
Since “podcast” is an English loanword in Romanian, it keeps the same form and meaning, making it easy to remember.
Connect “podcast” with your favorite internet broadcast to recall the blend of “iPod” and “broadcast.”
